newsSince: No material new company-specific news since June 9. Q1 2026 results and 2026 guidance raise (now EUR36-40B) were April; the Musk Terafab headline that drove the early-June rerating remains unsigned. ASML is essentially flat since June 9 (+0.08%).

sources.label: InsiderFinance: ASML bookings surge, lifts 2026 guidance

verdicts.rankRationale: AI-node lens: monopoly EUV choke point intact but score already capped for crowding/valuation discovery. No new signed orders or guidance change since June 9; the Terafab catalyst stays a headline. No fresh alpha to add.
verdicts.bull: Irreplaceable EUV/High-NA monopoly; raised 2026 sales guidance EUR36-40B; AI capex tailwind multi-year.
verdicts.neutral: Thesis intact but fully discounted; flat since June 9, no new orders.
verdicts.bear: +53% YTD, record $687B cap; crowding and China-restriction overhang cap residual alpha.

verdicts.rankRationale: CPO lens: ASML's link to co-packaged optics is indirect (via advanced nodes/packaging flows). Nothing since June 9 changes that linkage; ranked deep at 70, choke-point centrality intact but residual CPO alpha unchanged.
verdicts.bull: Lithography enables every advanced node feeding CPO/packaging roadmaps.
verdicts.neutral: CPO exposure indirect; no new evidence either way since June 9.
verdicts.bear: Not a direct optics play; size and crowding limit alpha on this lens.

verdicts.rankRationale: Under-owned-bottleneck lens penalizes names that have already rerated; ASML has (+53% YTD, record cap). No new bottleneck evidence since June 9, so no basis to re-rate the residual alpha higher; hold the prior down-positioning.
verdicts.bull: Scarcest pick-and-shovel choke point in the AI supply chain.
verdicts.neutral: Bottleneck real but no longer under-owned; thesis stable.
verdicts.bear: Record market cap on an unsigned headline; minimal residual alpha for this list.
